title: Death
content:
Nor dread nor hope attend
A dying animal;
A man awaits his end
Dreading and hoping all;
Many times he died,
Many times rose again.
A great man in his pride
Confronting murderous men
Casts derision upon
Supersession of breath;
He knows death to the bone --
Man has created death.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Butler Yeats delivers a powerful performance in "Death"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
